The Most Difficult CEO
Skill You Must Master
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 1 of 50 --

BY FAR THE HARDEST SKILL YOU MUST
LEARN AS A CEO IS THE SKILL OF
MANAGING YOUR OWN PSYCHOLOGY
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 50 --

ORGANIZATIONAL DESIGN,
COMMUNICATION,
HIRING/FIRING AND
PROMOTIONS ARE NOTHING
COMPARED TO KEEPING YOUR
MENTAL SPACE CLEAN.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 50 --

MANY OF YOU MAY RELATE TO THIS STATEMENT
“IF THINGS ARE GOING SO WELL WHY DO I FEEL SO
BADLY?”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 50 --

You also must be ….
Accomplished enough
Aware enough
Smart enough
That other people will want to work for you….
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 50 --

Therefore feeling you are doing a bad
job (which is normal) does not feel
usual to you...
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 6 of 50 --

The first realization you must accept is
that everybody learns how to be a CEO
by BEING A CEO
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 7 of 50 --

The only thing that prepares you for
running a company….
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 8 of 50 --

IS RUNNING A DAMN COMPANY
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 9 of 50 --

Inevitably, this means that you will
face a broad spectrum of problems
that
	you don’t know how to solve that
will require a skillset you don’t have...
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 10 of 50 --

Oh, and everybody will expect you to
know how and have the skills, because
you are CEO!
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 11 of 50 --

Going hand in hand, even if you grasp
this concept you must also understand
the second concept which is that….
even if you know what you’re doing,
things will go wrong anyway.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 12 of 50 --

Why??
Because you are in a multifaceted
HUMAN organization looking to
compete and WIN in a competitive
marketplace….
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 13 of 50 --

You may feel many days that your
performance is a 2/10...and due to the
nature of who you are that is extremely
hard to accept.
Accept it anyways.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 14 of 50 --

The ironic truth is that every single
thing that goes wrong in your
organization is your fault.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 15 of 50 --

The rack is shakey
Your admin isn’t following up
The leads aren’t coming in fast enough
Your YELP reviews went down
Your coach showed up late to 5am
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 16 of 50 --

Yep. Every problem is 100% your fault.
As the owner and CEO every hire and
decision ever made happened under
YOUR WATCH
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 17 of 50 --

So it’s natural that feeling you’re a 2/10
and f**king up everything starts to
wear on you….
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 18 of 50 --

Here is where most CEO’s go wrong...
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 19 of 50 --

1. They take things too personally
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 20 of 50 --

1. They take things too personally
2. They do not take things personally
enough
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 21 of 50 --

They take things too personally
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 22 of 50 --

They take things too personally
● You take every issue incredibly personally and seriously and
PERSONALLY attempt to fix it all
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 23 of 50 --

They take things too personally
● You take every issue incredibly personally and seriously and
PERSONALLY attempt to fix it all
○ Resulting in suppressing the team
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 24 of 50 --

They take things too personally
● You take every issue incredibly personally and seriously and
PERSONALLY attempt to fix it all
○ Resulting in suppressing the team
○ Or feeling so terrible on a daily basis you can’t get out of bed
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 25 of 50 --

You don’t take things personally enough
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 26 of 50 --

You don’t take things personally enough
● In an attempt to mask the pain, you take on an attitude of ‘it’s not
so bad’ or ‘it’s not a big deal’
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 27 of 50 --

You don’t take things personally enough
● In an attempt to mask the pain, you take on an attitude of ‘it’s not
so bad’ or ‘it’s not a big deal’
○ Suddenly no situation requires urgency or attention
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 28 of 50 --

You don’t take things personally enough
● In an attempt to mask the pain, you take on an attitude of ‘it’s not
so bad’ or ‘it’s not a big deal’
○ Suddenly no situation requires urgency or attention
○ Team is frustrated you don’t take situations to heart and
don’t help support them to fix problems
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 29 of 50 --

You don’t take things personally enough
● In an attempt to mask the pain, you take on an attitude of ‘it’s not
so bad’ or ‘it’s not a big deal’
○ Suddenly no situation requires urgency or attention
○ Team is frustrated you don’t take situations to heart and
don’t help support them to fix problems
○ You feel better...
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 30 of 50 --

Ideally, you need to be urgent but not
psychotic.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 31 of 50 --

You need to be able to move
aggressively and decisively without
feeling emotionally guilty.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 32 of 50 --

If you can seperate the importance and
urgency of these issues from how you
feel...you will avoid your team freaking
out or feeling like crap as well as
yourself.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 33 of 50 --

If you can’t accept and deal with those
facts you are in the wrong position.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 34 of 50 --

Feeling like a failure and coming close
to losing is NORMAL in business and as
a CEO.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 35 of 50 --

So how do you master the skill of
managing your state?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 36 of 50 --

Everyone is different but in my time
overseeing this group and mentoring
others these techniques are universally
applicable...
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 37 of 50 --

1. Make friends, have friends
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 38 of 50 --

1. Make friends, have friends
a. You ARE in a group of about 900 others who can
relate…be a friend to make a friend
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 39 of 50 --

1. Make friends, have friends
a. You ARE in a group of about 900 others who can
relate…be a friend to make a friend
2. Stay in your head you’re dead
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 40 of 50 --

1. Make friends, have friends
a. You ARE in a group of about 900 others who can
relate…be a friend to make a friend
2. Stay in your head you’re dead
a. Write down the problems in your head, suddenly they
become more manageable
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 41 of 50 --

1. Make friends, have friends
a. You ARE in a group of about 900 others who can
relate…be a friend to make a friend
2. Stay in your head you’re dead
a. Write down the problems in your head, suddenly they
become more manageable
3. Focus on the road not the wall
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 42 of 50 --

1. Make friends, have friends
a. You ARE in a group of about 900 others who can
relate…be a friend to make a friend
2. Stay in your head you’re dead
a. Write down the problems in your head, suddenly they
become more manageable
3. Focus on the road not the wall
a. There are a thousand things around you that can sink
the ship, focus on where you are sailing and stop
giving attention to what can go wrong
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 43 of 50 --

Remember, everyone wants to quit.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 44 of 50 --

Many will try to cope with drugs,
alcohol, sex, netflix, or some other self
detrimental mechanism
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 45 of 50 --

And then one day quit, claiming it was
the stress of the job that drove them to
destroy themselves…
….so they had to quit
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 46 of 50 --

Great CEOs face the pain.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 47 of 50 --

Great CEOs face the pain.
Great CEOs confront the hard stuff.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 48 of 50 --

Great CEOs face the pain.
Great CEOs confront the hard stuff.
Great CEOs deal with the sleepless nights.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 49 of 50 --

Great CEOs face the pain.
Great CEOs confront the hard stuff.
Great CEOs deal with the sleepless nights.
Great CEOs don’t quit.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 50 of 50 --
